topic: Is 16-LG1 (worker results don't propagate) critical or just high?
rationale: BFT 2/3 supermajority confirms critical: a real-LangGraph deployment hits ValidationError on first worker fan-out. Mock graph hides it.
dissent_notes: A05 noted that severity depends on whether tests use the mock or real graph; quorum overrode.

topic: Is 22-C1 (BFT unanimity at n=3) a real bug or by-design?
rationale: PBFT cannot tolerate any fault with n=3; the textbook formula requires n>=4. The existing implementation produces unanimity-required behaviour silently. Recommend explicit n>=4 guard.
dissent_notes: A23 argued that documenting was sufficient; quorum demanded a code change.

topic: Is 17-CORR1 (string-equality vote bucketing) critical or high?
rationale: Unanimous: in any LLM-driven swarm, semantically-equivalent paraphrases will defeat consensus. Affects all 4 protocols. Requires semantic clustering (cosine on embeddings) which is a 1wk effort.

topic: Is 19-SEC1 (no single-use approval guard) critical or high?
rationale: BFT 2/3: replay vulnerability in HITL is a critical safety issue. ai-coder already implements approval_consumed; hive-swarm does not.
dissent_notes: A05 suggested 'high' because no production deployment yet; A19 and A20 prevailed.

topic: Is 25-CORR1 (topology label vs runtime mismatch) critical or design-by-intent?
rationale: Queen (A05) breaks tie: docs and code disagree fundamentally. Either rename topologies to role_set_* OR implement real ring/mesh/star.
dissent_notes: A02 argued the parallel fan-out IS a valid simplification; queen overruled because the synthesis report explicitly claims topology-distinct behaviour.

topic: Is 20-SEC1 (toy redaction regex) critical given the docstring admits it?
rationale: BFT 2/3: even with a docstring caveat, default behaviour leaks AWS keys / GitHub PATs / Bearer tokens. ai-coder's real Redactor exists; should be the default.
dissent_notes: A02 said docstring shifts blame to user; A20 and A12 prevailed (defaults matter).

topic: Is 29-CORR1 + 29-CORR2 (quota tracker non-atomic + no flock) one critical bug or two?
rationale: 3/4 majority: two distinct fixes (atomic write AND flock) are required; collapsing them risks half-fix. Track separately.
dissent_notes: A02 suggested SQLite migration solves both at once; majority preferred independent fixes for incremental rollout.
